notes:
bám = to stick to or cling to something so it doesn’t fall off or get separated.

It’s a verb. You often use it for things physically attaching (dirt, glue, insects) or a person/animal holding tightly onto something. It usually goes with bám vào + object when talking about contact.

It can also be more figurative: bám theo (follow closely, almost cling to someone) or bám sát (stick close, follow closely in space or ideas).  
Close to dính (stick), but bám often feels more active, like holding on.
exampleSentencesTranslated: The little child likes to stick to his mother's hand when going outside.
Mud will stick to your shoes after walking in the rain.
Please clean the window because there is a lot of dust stick on it.
notes_md:
**bám** = to stick to or cling to something so it doesn’t fall off or get separated.

It’s a verb. You often use it for things physically attaching (dirt, glue, insects) or a person/animal holding tightly onto something. It usually goes with **bám vào + object** when talking about contact.


It can also be more figurative: **bám theo** (follow closely, almost cling to someone) or **bám sát** (stick close, follow closely in space or ideas).  
Close to **dính** (stick), but *bám* often feels more active, like holding on.
exampleSentences: Em nhỏ thích bám vào tay mẹ khi đi ra ngoài.
Bùn bám vào giày anh sau khi đi dưới mưa.
Chị hãy lau sạch cửa sổ vì có nhiều bụi bám trên đó.

example.vi: Con mèo bám vào chân tôi.
example.en: The cat clung to my leg.
example.es: El gato se aferró a mi pierna.

definitions.en: To cling or stick to something.
definitions.vi: Bám vào hoặc dính vào một cái gì đó.
